content: yeah I know you think things through pretty extensively, was mostly just that the tone of the statement was a bit too aggressive rather than the idea behind it, if that makes sense
The primary example being this "some style guide somewhere" which I am already in the process of writing

For sure. I've been struggling my entire life with my natural tones of voice tending to be overly aggressive or patronizing <:PAIMON_DEAD:708170044848472105> I'm really sorry when that comes through, especially in text. Again, I apologize. <:LUV:1003188300036702229>
I hate that ever since goz pointed out it was a skull i just see a really ugly skull now instead of a cute frown face minighost
also on the topic of AI bad, fuck you too google
Feels like AI is included in every browser nowadays, good that you can turn it off at least

That would be neat, actually
For example there was a semi-quote from "Nemesis" in Sorel's story where ||N opens a black portal||, I deduced it even from GPT. There were literally lines of "dark universe yawning, where black planets roll without aim"

Also regarding Clem, in RU we've ditched the mention of Shan/Shaggai completely and left "insect/moth" since it'd be like calling a Mi-Go a Yuggoth.
And tbh their MO in chapter, and Clem being designed after an Acherontia moth instead of a more conventional insect, differ greatly from Shan
Also the idea that we specifically edited out every mention of R'lyeh since it's Lemuria in canon, just as Miskatonic is Mythag.
Part 2 confuses stuff a bit, though, since ||there's no Mythag but there is Miskatonic, but Lemuria is still Lemuria nonetheless.|| Idk, it takes some brainwork to piece it out

Alright, so I sent a barrage of text to Light and I'm super happy to say he got back to me and answered what he could! I'll only share what's relevant to the project for now as I did tell him whatever he tells me would be on a need-to-know basis. **Consider anything I'm about to say as covered under the NDA.**

First, he said that large-scale revisions of game mechanics (like those in JP server) are not currently planned. I take this to mean I/we should be safe to regard the basic functions of the game as stable ground. (i.e. turns vs stacks of Vulnerable)

Second, in regards to progress on new hires and the roadmap for EN translation, BIAV **has** added a new professional translator to their team, but did not mention who, or whether we would be able to communicate with them. He also mentioned that with the short-term aid of a translation agency, current progress has reached **Chapter 6** and they're on track for their goal for **V2.4**; 

He said their rough timeline for translation is Arc 1 main story first, then maintaining localization for newly updated content, then gradually re-translating side story content after that. Whether they intend to continue working with the agency past Arc 1 was not mentioned. 

Third, he confirmed my guess that Ace is very busy, and simply does not have the time to engage with and answer every question we have, so he's grateful for anyone who has stepped up in this project to help fill those gaps.

Finally, as for my personal project of revamping the gameplay text of the EN version, he said they initially had no expectations of the volunteer project yielding significant results on that front, as the CN version is also in need of standardization work, however;
(1) he estimates it'll be another few months at least before they can allocate the resources for that, BUT
(2) he agrees with me that the EN version doesn't need to be dragged behind for that reason, and that having a solid benchmark in EN will help reduce distortion from translation iteration, so he's given me the **OK to proceed with my redux!**

Just curious, if anyone here doing translation into other languages need a sheet for Lovecraftian elements appearing in game
Like if there’s a sentence quoting directly from Lovecraft’s works in CN, you guys can search for the corresponding work/translations in EN/other languages of that work, and remain the quoting style as some lore rabbit holes
I’ll consider to make a sheet about this if there’s anyone interested
honestly just a collection of which things are direct references to Lovecraft *at all* would be helpful lmao
I have not directly read Lovecraft because as a proud uncultured swine, I of course do not read.
<:jenkin_processing:1408324289115062333>

Many of those references appear in Awakener/WoD stories which mostly don’t have a EN translation yet
but there are some appear in Enlighten/skill names/enemies
which might be close to what is worked on recently lmao

I know there are a toooooon of references, and I do get most of them, and I have a passing-grade understanding of the wider lore but the specifics sometimes I miss.

Like I knew "Shaggai" had something to do with insects, but until I went to look it up I did not know that "Shaggai" is the name of the planet that the insectoid people called *Shan* are from.
That being said though, it would be VERY helpful to have that cross-reference sheet available, so that we could figure out once and for all whether Clementine is actually a worm or a snake.

Ah this one is actually a fun coincidence 
in CN we call that insectoid creature “insects from Shaggai” or just “Shaggai”, due to translation circumstances of the original Cthulhu Mythos works (there is a story called “Insects from Shaggai” widely spread in CN) and there’s not even a name for “Shan” that does not involve “Shaggai” in CN
so it’s clear enough for CN player that Clem is a zerg-like entities, but not so for EN player with “Shaggai” involved instead of “Shan”
There are a few more such coincidences like this, mostly generated from CN translation of Lovecraftian works, which is why I think making such references clear is necessary.

Stuff like this makes me feel like this project is still kinda in the setup stage. Everyone got really enthusiastic right at the start and then quickly realized that we actually have to begin with organization and research tools before we can put together a truly worthwhile translation
But is fun!

very nice but I still stand by my original stance, weakness/vul need to be stacks instead of turns because every single a1 madness difficulty boss fight (which is the significant majority of events ingame) and every major chapter boss in the game from ch6 onwards reduce vul/weakness stacks by 2 per turn. Saying "turns" of vul/weakness would be objectively incorrect in a significant amount of important game content (with main story chapter bosses generally being the first real bottleneck that newer players encounter in progression, so ensuring accuracy and clarity of effects in those fights is key)

actually you know what, I was going to point to the existing ingame descriptions as an example, only to discover that most vul effects ingame do in fact specify "turns" instead of "stacks" (despite that being objectively wrong in a lot of fights!!!) so uh, fuck it actually if you wanna make it turns let's make it turns
neat to see that turns/stacks can be inconsistent *within the same kit* though, love to see it

side note: I have discovered why I disliked "turns" so much, and it's because I use aigis on most of my teams and she can inflict 99+ stacks of vul and saying "99 turns of vulnerable" just sounds silly tbh

also last note, I still prefer stacks simply because the vulnerability tooltip itself does specify one stack removed per turn so there's no unclearness or vagueness as to how vulnerability works, plus the nature of a stacking debuff simply lends itself to the word stack much more intuitively

note that should vulnerable be replaced with turns, the wording of this card (and aigis's kit in general) becomes quite awkward - "increases by 5% for each turn of vulnerable" - what does that mean, exactly? does it mean the number of turns that have passed in the battle where the enemy was vulnerable'd? what is the risk of someone reading it like that compared to the 100% clear wording of "for each stack of vulnerable"?

Vulnerable is a debuff status that has stacks. When referring to a status that has stacks, we use the word stacks.

Vulnerable is a debuff status that inherently is reduced by 1 stack at the end of each turn. When INFLICTING a status that behaves this way, we describe that effect as inflicting "x turn(s)" of the status.

We don't refer to a stack of Vulnerable as "a turn of Vulnerable" outside of the effect that inflicts it. Bosses would "remove an extra stack of (weak/vuln) each turn".

The intuition problem here is that unlike basically every other status in this game that stacks, there is in a vast majority of cases no effective difference between the enemy having 1 stack of Weakness and having 300 stacks of Weakness, aside from how long the debuff lasts. We must make that distinction clear to the player lest they believe that "inflicting 3 stacks of Vulnerable" somehow lets them deal more damage to the enemy this turn than inflicting just 1. This is the end of the discussion.
your point is valid, but as a side note, statements like "This is the end of the discussion." are quite detrimental towards topics of discussion and create somewhat of a hostile environment
I don't think sentences like that are necessary or, frankly, very contributory in general
anyway that's a fair distinction to make and works for me, but should probably be detailed in some style guide somewhere if we want to standardize all instances of wording for it

I understand and agree with that but at a certain point we gotta trust each other to make good decisions and not get bogged down on little details like that. I apologize for using it that way and I for sure am not going to make a habit of it. I'm guilty myself of over-debating so I'm no Saint but I'm getting better

In hindsight what I should have said would be closer to "trust me, I got this", as I wanted to communicate that I'm 100% confident I've assessed every variable and possibility and have made the best decision, and further discussing the topic would simply mean wasting airspace describing what I'm already intending to do; when actually doing it would be the same amount of effort and give better results.
